name: Interpolated PVCs
identifyBy: PVC occurs between two normal sinus beats WITHOUT a compensatory pause
Both flanking sinus beats conduct normally - PVC is 'sandwiched'
PR interval of the beat AFTER the PVC may be slightly prolonged (retrograde concealed conduction)
Occurs at slower sinus rates - PVC 'fits' between beats without displacing them
Rate must be slow enough to allow the PVC to occur without disrupting the next P wave

measurements.pWaves: Normal sinus P waves before and after PVC
measurements.prInterval: Slightly prolonged after interpolated PVC (concealed retrograde conduction)

management: As for standard PVCs - benign in structurally normal heart
No specific additional treatment beyond standard PVC management
pearls: Interpolated PVCs occur when the sinus rate is slow enough to allow a PVC without displacing sinus rhythm
The slightly prolonged PR after the PVC is pathognomonic - retrograde conduction into the AV node causes transient refractoriness
Unlike standard PVCs: NO compensatory pause - sinus rhythm continues uninterrupted
May be missed if not looking carefully - only identified by the absence of the expected compensatory pause
differentials: PAC with aberrant conduction (non-compensatory pause, different P morphology)
